message.content:
Explore the Android project at E:\AndroidProjects\FrameLog and give me a complete picture of:
1. The full directory tree under app/src/main/java/com/southsouthwest/framelog/ (all files and folders)
2. The content of app/src/main/java/com/southsouthwest/framelog/MainActivity.kt
3. The content of gradle/libs.versions.toml (specifically navigation, compose, material3 versions)
4. The content of app/build.gradle or app/build.gradle.kts (dependencies section)
5. The content of any existing navigation-related files (look for NavGraph, Navigation, nav in filenames)
6. The content of app/src/main/res/values/strings.xml if it exists

List every .kt file in the project tree.

1â†’package com.southsouthwest.framelog.ui.theme
     2â†’
     3â†’import android.app.Activity
     4â†’import android.os.Build
     5â†’import androidx.compose.foundation.isSystemInDarkTheme
     6â†’import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
     7â†’import androidx.compose.material3.darkColorScheme
     8â†’import androidx.compose.material3.dynamicDarkColorScheme
     9â†’import androidx.compose.material3.dynamicLightColorScheme
    10â†’import androidx.compose.material3.lightColorScheme
    11â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
    12â†’import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
    13â†’
    14â†’private val DarkColorScheme = darkColorScheme(
    15â†’    primary = Purple80,
    16â†’    secondary = PurpleGrey80,
    17â†’    tertiary = Pink80
    18â†’)
    19â†’
    20â†’private val LightColorScheme = lightColorScheme(
    21â†’    primary = Purple40,
    22â†’    secondary = PurpleGrey40,
    23â†’    tertiary = Pink40
    24â†’
    25â†’    /* Other default colors to override
    26â†’    background = Color(0xFFFFFBFE),
    27â†’    surface = Color(0xFFFFFBFE),
    28â†’    onPrimary = Color.White,
    29â†’    onSecondary = Color.White,
    30â†’    onTertiary = Color.White,
    31â†’    onBackground = Color(0xFF1C1B1F),
    32â†’    onSurface = Color(0xFF1C1B1F),
    33â†’    */
    34â†’)
    35â†’
    36â†’@Composable
    37â†’fun FrameLogTheme(
    38â†’    darkTheme: Boolean = isSystemInDarkTheme(),
    39â†’    // Dynamic color is available on Android 12+
    40â†’    dynamicColor: Boolean = true,
    41â†’    content: @Composable () -> Unit
    42â†’) {
    43â†’    val colorScheme = when {
    44â†’        dynamicColor && Build.VERSION.SDK_INT >= Build.VERSION_CODES.S -> {
    45â†’            val context = LocalContext.current
    46â†’            if (darkTheme) dynamicDarkColorScheme(context) else dynamicLightColorScheme(context)
    47â†’        }
    48â†’
    49â†’        darkTheme -> DarkColorScheme
    50â†’        else -> LightColorScheme
    51â†’    }
    52â†’
    53â†’    MaterialTheme(
    54â†’        colorScheme = colorScheme,
    55â†’        typography = Typography,
    56â†’        content = content
    57â†’    )
    58â†’}

content.text: Perfect! Now let me create a comprehensive summary. Let me read a few more sample ViewModel and entity files to give you a complete picture:

content.content:
1â†’package com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity
     2â†’
     3â†’import androidx.room.Entity
     4â†’import androidx.room.ForeignKey
     5â†’import androidx.room.Index
     6â†’import androidx.room.PrimaryKey
     7â†’
     8â†’/**
     9â†’ * A roll of film â€” the primary shooting journal entity.
    10â†’ *
    11â†’ * [isLoaded] distinguishes rolls in a camera (true) from inventory rolls (false). Multiple
    12â†’ * rolls can be loaded simultaneously (one per camera body). The widget and quick screen reflect
    13â†’ * whichever loaded roll the user has selected as active â€” that selection is stored in
    14â†’ * SharedPreferences, not here.
    15â†’ *
    16â†’ * [status] and [isLoaded] are independent:
    17â†’ *   - active + unloaded: purchased, in inventory, not yet in a camera
    18â†’ *   - active + loaded: in a camera, actively shooting
    19â†’ *   - finished: roll is done, camera unloaded
    20â†’ *   - archived: finished roll moved to archive
    21â†’ *
    22â†’ * [pushPull] drives [ratedISO]: ratedISO = FilmStock.iso Ă— 2^pushPull.
    23â†’ * null pushPull indicates a custom ISO override â€” the user has set ratedISO manually.
    24â†’ *
    25â†’ * [totalExposures] is immutable after creation. Calculated at roll creation:
    26â†’ *   - Standard body: defaultFrameCount + extraFrames
    27â†’ *   - Half-frame body: (defaultFrameCount + extraFrames) Ă— 2
    28â†’ * All [Frame] slots are bulk-created at roll creation to match this count.
    29â†’ */
    30â†’@Entity(
    31â†’    tableName = "rolls",
    32â†’    foreignKeys = [
    33â†’        ForeignKey(
    34â†’            entity = FilmStock::class,
    35â†’            parentColumns = ["id"],
    36â†’            childColumns = ["filmStockId"],
    37â†’        ),
    38â†’        ForeignKey(
    39â†’            entity = CameraBody::class,
    40â†’            parentColumns = ["id"],
    41â†’            childColumns = ["cameraBodyId"],
    42â†’        ),
    43â†’    ],
    44â†’    indices = [Index("filmStockId"), Index("cameraBodyId")],
    45â†’)
    46â†’data class Roll(
    47â†’    @PrimaryKey(autoGenerate = true) val id: Int = 0,
    48â†’    val name: String,
    49â†’    val filmStockId: Int,
    50â†’    val cameraBodyId: Int,
    51â†’    /**
    52â†’     * Push/pull stops. Range -3 to +3, default 0 (box speed).
    53â†’     * Null when the user has entered a custom ratedISO override.
    54â†’     */
    55â†’    val pushPull: Int? = 0,

    57â†’    val ratedISO: Int,

    59â†’    val filmExpiryDate: Long? = null,

1â†’package com.southsouthwest.framelog.data.db.entity
     2â†’
     3â†’import androidx.room.Entity
     4â†’import androidx.room.ForeignKey
     5â†’import androidx.room.Index
     6â†’import androidx.room.PrimaryKey
     7â†’
     8â†’/**
     9â†’ * A single exposure slot on a roll.
    10â†’ *
    11â†’ * All frames are pre-created in bulk when a roll is loaded â€” one for each of
    12â†’ * Roll.totalExposures. An unlogged frame has isLogged = false and null exposure fields.
    13â†’ *
    14â†’ * [frameNumber] is immutable. Set at roll creation, never changes. This is the canonical
    15â†’ * ordering key for frames.
    16â†’ *
    17â†’ * [isLogged] and [loggedAt] are deliberately decoupled: a frame can be marked as logged
    18â†’ * retroactively without fabricating a timestamp. isLogged = true with loggedAt = null is
    19â†’ * valid (retroactive log). isLogged = false with loggedAt = null is an unlogged slot.
    20â†’ *
    21â†’ * [aperture] and [shutterSpeed] are stored as canonical strings (e.g. "f/5.6", "1/125").
    22â†’ * Valid values are enforced by the UI steppers, not the database.
    23â†’ *
    24â†’ * Active filters are in the FrameFilter join table, not here. Filters stack.
    25â†’ */
    26â†’@Entity(
    27â†’    tableName = "frames",
    28â†’    foreignKeys = [
    29â†’        ForeignKey(
    30â†’            entity = Roll::class,
    31â†’            parentColumns = ["id"],
    32â†’            childColumns = ["rollId"],
    33â†’            onDelete = ForeignKey.CASCADE,
    34â†’        ),
    35â†’        ForeignKey(
    36â†’            entity = Lens::class,
    37â†’            parentColumns = ["id"],
    38â†’            childColumns = ["lensId"],
    39â†’        ),
    40â†’    ],
    41â†’    indices = [Index("rollId"), Index("lensId")],
    42â†’)
    43â†’data class Frame(
    44â†’    @PrimaryKey(autoGenerate = true) val id: Int = 0,
    45â†’    val rollId: Int,
    46â†’    /** Immutable â€” 1 through Roll.totalExposures, set at roll creation. */
    47â†’    val frameNumber: Int,
    48â†’    val isLogged: Boolean = false,
    49â†’    /** When the frame was logged, as epoch milliseconds. Null if never logged or logged retroactively. */
    50â†’    val loggedAt: Long? = null,
    51â†’    /** Canonical string, e.g. "f/5.6". Null if unlogged. */
    52â†’    val aperture: String? = null,
    53â†’    /** Canonical string, e.g. "1/125", "1s", "B". Null if unlogged. */
    54â†’    val shutterSpeed: String? = null,
    55â†’    /** Must reference a lens in this roll's RollLens table. Enforced at application layer. */
    56â†’    val lensId: Int? = null,
    57â†’    /** Exposure compensation in EV stops, e.g. -1.0, +0.5. Null if not applied. */
    58â†’    val exposureCompensation: Float? = null,
    59â†’    /** GPS latitude. Captured automatically if Roll.gpsEnabled = true. */
    60â†’    val lat: Double? = null,
    61â†’    /** GPS longitude. Captured automatically if Roll.gpsEnabled = true. */
    62â†’    val lng: Double? = null,
    63â†’    val notes: String? = null,
    64â†’)
    65â†’

1â†’package com.southsouthwest.framelog.data
     2â†’
     3â†’import android.content.Context
     4â†’import androidx.core.content.edit
     5â†’
     6â†’private const val PREFS_NAME = "framelog_prefs"
     7â†’
     8â†’// Global keys
     9â†’private const val KEY_ONBOARDING_COMPLETE = "onboarding_complete"
    10â†’private const val KEY_ACTIVE_ROLL_ID = "active_roll_id"
    11â†’private const val KEY_EXTRA_FRAMES_PER_ROLL = "extra_frames_per_roll"
    12â†’private const val KEY_GPS_PRECISION = "gps_precision"
    13â†’private const val KEY_DEFAULT_EXPORT_FORMAT = "default_export_format"
    14â†’private const val KEY_APP_THEME = "app_theme"
    15â†’private const val KEY_ACCESSIBLE_COLOR_MODE = "accessible_color_mode"
    16â†’private const val KEY_TIP_JAR_PROMPT_SHOWN = "tip_jar_prompt_shown"
    17â†’
    18â†’// Per-roll keys
    19â†’private fun keyCurrentFrame(rollId: Int) = "current_frame_$rollId"
    20â†’private fun keyMruFilters(rollId: Int) = "mru_filters_$rollId"
    21â†’
    22â†’/**
    23â†’ * Thin wrapper around SharedPreferences for all app configuration.
    24â†’ *
    25â†’ * All photography data lives in Room. This class holds:
    26â†’ *   - The active roll ID (which loaded roll drives the widget and Quick Screen)
    27â†’ *   - Onboarding and session state
    28â†’ *   - User preferences (extra frames, GPS precision, export format, theme)
    29â†’ *   - Per-roll ephemeral state (current frame pointer, MRU filter chip order)
    30â†’ *
    31â†’ * Not a singleton â€” instantiate wherever needed with an Application or activity Context.
    32â†’ */
    33â†’class AppPreferences(context: Context) {
    34â†’
    35â†’    private val prefs = context.applicationContext
    36â†’        .getSharedPreferences(PREFS_NAME, Context.MODE_PRIVATE)
    37â†’
    38â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    39â†’    // Onboarding
    40â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    41â†’
    42â†’    /** True after the user completes or skips onboarding. Gates the coach mark flow. */
    43â†’    var onboardingComplete: Boolean
    44â†’        get() = prefs.getBoolean(KEY_ONBOARDING_COMPLETE, false)
    45â†’        set(value) = prefs.edit { putBoolean(KEY_ONBOARDING_COMPLETE, value) }
    46â†’
    47â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    48â†’    // Active roll selection
    49â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    50â†’
    51â†’    /**
    52â†’     * The roll ID currently selected for the widget and Quick Screen.
    53â†’     * -1 means no roll is selected (no loaded rolls exist).
    54â†’     *
    55â†’     * Updated by:
    56â†’     *   - RollSetup when "Create & Load Roll" is tapped (if no other loaded roll exists)
    57â†’     *   - Quick Screen switch-roll sheet when user selects a different loaded roll
    58â†’     *   - Automatically when the active roll is finished or unloaded (falls back to next loaded roll)
    59â†’     */
    60â†’    var activeRollId: Int
    61â†’        get() = prefs.getInt(KEY_ACTIVE_ROLL_ID, -1)
    62â†’        set(value) = prefs.edit { putInt(KEY_ACTIVE_ROLL_ID, value) }
    63â†’
    64â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    65â†’    // Shooting defaults
    66â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    67â†’
    68â†’    /**
    69â†’     * Extra frames appended beyond the film stock's defaultFrameCount at roll creation.
    70â†’     * The extra frames account for leader frames and the slight over-capacity of most film.
    71â†’     * Default 2. Editable in Settings.
    72â†’     */
    73â†’    var extraFramesPerRoll: Int
    74â†’        get() = prefs.getInt(KEY_EXTRA_FRAMES_PER_ROLL, 2)
    75â†’        set(value) = prefs.edit { putInt(KEY_EXTRA_FRAMES_PER_ROLL, value) }
    76â†’
    77â†’    var gpsPrecision: GpsPrecision
    78â†’        get() = GpsPrecision.fromKey(prefs.getString(KEY_GPS_PRECISION, GpsPrecision.HIGH.key)!!)
    79â†’        set(value) = prefs.edit { putString(KEY_GPS_PRECISION, value.key) }
    80â†’
    81â†’    var defaultExportFormat: ExportFormat
    82â†’        get() = ExportFormat.fromKey(
    83â†’            prefs.getString(KEY_DEFAULT_EXPORT_FORMAT, ExportFormat.CSV.key)!!
    84â†’        )
    85â†’        set(value) = prefs.edit { putString(KEY_DEFAULT_EXPORT_FORMAT, value.key) }
    86â†’
    87â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    88â†’    // Appearance
    89â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    90â†’
    91â†’    var appTheme: AppTheme
    92â†’        get() = AppTheme.fromKey(prefs.getString(KEY_APP_THEME, AppTheme.SYSTEM.key)!!)
    93â†’        set(value) = prefs.edit { putString(KEY_APP_THEME, value.key) }
    94â†’
    95â†’    /**
    96â†’     * When true, color-coded elements (e.g. long-exposure shutter speeds) use a supplementary
    97â†’     * indicator (underline or suffix) in addition to or instead of color alone.
    98â†’     */
    99â†’    var accessibleColorMode: Boolean
   100â†’        get() = prefs.getBoolean(KEY_ACCESSIBLE_COLOR_MODE, false)
   101â†’        set(value) = prefs.edit { putBoolean(KEY_ACCESSIBLE_COLOR_MODE, value) }
   102â†’
   103â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   104â†’    // Tip jar
   105â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   106â†’
   107â†’    /**
   108â†’     * True once the one-time 5-rolls-logged prompt has been shown.
   109â†’     * The tip jar button in Settings remains visible regardless of this flag.
   110â†’     */
   111â†’    var tipJarPromptShown: Boolean
   112â†’        get() = prefs.getBoolean(KEY_TIP_JAR_PROMPT_SHOWN, false)
   113â†’        set(value) = prefs.edit { putBoolean(KEY_TIP_JAR_PROMPT_SHOWN, value) }
   114â†’
   115â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   116â†’    // Per-roll state
   117â†’    // ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   118â†’
   119â†’    /**
   120â†’     * The current frame number for a roll (1-based). This is the frame the Quick Screen
   121â†’     * and widget are pointing at for the next log operation.
   122â†’     *
   123â†’     * Initialized to 1 when a roll is first loaded. Updated after each successful frame log
   124â†’     * (advances to the next unlogged frame) and by the frame pointer stepper.
   125â†’     */
   126â†’    fun getCurrentFrameNumber(rollId: Int): Int =
   127â†’        prefs.getInt(keyCurrentFrame(rollId), 1)
   128â†’
   129â†’    fun setCurrentFrameNumber(rollId: Int, frameNumber: Int) =
   130â†’        prefs.edit { putInt(keyCurrentFrame(rollId), frameNumber) }
   131â†’
   132â†’    /**
   133â†’     * Ordered list of up to 4 most recently toggled filter IDs for a roll.
   134â†’     * Drives the filter chip row on the Quick Screen and Frame Detail screen.
   135â†’     * Order: most recently used first (index 0).
   136â†’     */
   137â†’    fun getMruFilterIds(rollId: Int): List<Int> {
   138â†’        val raw = prefs.getString(keyMruFilters(rollId), "") ?: ""
   139â†’        if (raw.isBlank()) return emptyList()
   140â†’        return raw.split(",").mapNotNull { it.toIntOrNull() }
   141â†’    }
   142â†’
   143â†’    fun setMruFilterIds(rollId: Int, filterIds: List<Int>) {
   144â†’        prefs.edit { putString(keyMruFilters(rollId), filterIds.take(4).joinToString(",")) }
   145â†’    }
   146â†’
   147â†’    /**
   148â†’     * Promotes [filterId] to the front of the MRU list for [rollId], keeping at most 4 entries.
   149â†’     * Called whenever the user toggles a filter on the Quick Screen or Frame Detail screen.
   150â†’     */
   151â†’    fun promoteFilterInMru(rollId: Int, filterId: Int) {
   152â†’        val current = getMruFilterIds(rollId).toMutableList()
   153â†’        current.remove(filterId)
   154â†’        current.add(0, filterId)
   155â†’        setMruFilterIds(rollId, current)
   156â†’    }
   157â†’
   158â†’    /**
   159â†’     * Removes all per-roll keys for the given roll. Called when a roll is deleted.
   160â†’     */
   161â†’    fun clearRollPreferences(rollId: Int) {
   162â†’        prefs.edit {
   163â†’            remove(keyCurrentFrame(rollId))
   164â†’            remove(keyMruFilters(rollId))
   165â†’        }
   166â†’    }
   167â†’}
   168â†’
   169â†’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   170â†’// Preference enums
   171â†’// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   172â†’
   173â†’enum class GpsPrecision(val key: String) {
   174â†’    HIGH("high"),
   175â†’    BATTERY_SAVER("battery_saver");
   176â†’
   177â†’    companion object {
   178â†’        fun fromKey(key: String) = entries.firstOrNull { it.key == key } ?: HIGH
   179â†’    }
   180â†’}
   181â†’
   182â†’enum class ExportFormat(val key: String) {
   183â†’    CSV("csv"),
   184â†’    JSON("json"),
   185â†’    PLAIN_TEXT("plain_text");
   186â†’
   187â†’    companion object {
   188â†’        fun fromKey(key: String) = entries.firstOrNull { it.key == key } ?: CSV
   189â†’    }
   190â†’}
   191â†’
   192â†’enum class AppTheme(val key: String) {
   193â†’    LIGHT("light"),
   194â†’    DARK("dark"),
   195â†’    SYSTEM("system");
   196â†’
   197â†’    companion object {
   198â†’        fun fromKey(key: String) = entries.firstOrNull { it.key == key } ?: SYSTEM
   199â†’    }
   200â†’}
   201â†’

### 2. MainActivity.kt Content

Located at: `/app/src/main/java/com/southsouthwest/framelog/MainActivity.kt`

Currently a stub with placeholder "Hello Android" Greeting text. Uses Jetpack Compose with Material3 Scaffold and FrameLogTheme. Enables edge-to-edge layout.

### 3. gradle/libs.versions.toml - Key Dependencies

**Version Catalog:**
- **Kotlin:** 2.2.10
- **Compose BOM:** 2024.09.00
- **Navigation Compose:** 2.8.0
- **Material 3:** (via compose-bom)
- **Room:** 2.7.0
- **KSP:** 2.2.10-2.0.2
- **Lifecycle ViewModel:** 2.8.0
- **Coroutines:** 1.8.1
- **kotlinx-serialization-json:** 1.7.3
- **Play Services Location:** 21.3.0
- **Android Gradle Plugin:** 9.1.0
- **Core KTX:** 1.10.1
- **Activity Compose:** 1.8.0

### 4. app/build.gradle.kts - Dependencies Section

**Key dependencies:**
- androidx.core:core-ktx (1.10.1)
- androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-runtime-ktx (2.6.1)
- androidx.activity:activity-compose (1.8.0)
- androidx.compose (via BOM) â€” ui, ui-graphics, ui-tooling-preview, material3
- androidx.room:room-runtime, room-ktx, room-compiler (2.7.0, KSP)
- androidx.navigation:navigation-compose (2.8.0)
- androidx.lifecycle:lifecycle-viewmodel-compose, lifecycle-viewmodel-ktx (2.8.0)
- kotlinx.coroutines:kotlinx-coroutines-android (1.8.1)
- kotlinx.serialization:kotlinx-serialization-json (1.7.3) â€” for type-safe Navigation 2.8+ routes
- com.google.android.gms:play-services-location (21.3.0) â€” GPS capture

**Build config:**
- Min SDK 26, Target/Compile SDK 36
- Java 11 compatibility
- KSP enabled with room.schemaLocation for schema export
- Compose enabled
- Serialization plugin enabled (kotlin-serialization)

### 5. Navigation Files

No dedicated navigation files found in src/main. The app uses **Jetpack Navigation Compose 2.8.0** with **type-safe routes** (kotlinx-serialization-json) but Composable screen files are not yet in the codebase. The architecture is set up to accept them.

Navigation documentation exists in `/docs/Screen Inventory/`:
- `FRAMELOG navigation map.dat`
- `FRAMELOG navigation map_me.svg`


---

## KEY ARCHITECTURE OBSERVATIONS

**Data Layer - Complete:**
- Room database with 13 entities (Camera Body, Lens, Filter, Film Stock, Kit, Roll, Frame + join tables)
- 11 DAOs + relationship objects (FrameWithDetails, KitWithDetails, RollWithDetails, RollExport)
- 4 Repository classes (GearRepository, RollRepository, FrameRepository, KitRepository)
- AppPreferences wrapper for SharedPreferences (onboarding, active roll, extra frames, GPS precision, theme, export format, tip jar tracking)

**UI Layer - ViewModel Structure:**
- 13 ViewModels already defined for all screen categories
- No Composable screen files yet (they will be added following MVVM pattern)
- Theme fully configured (Color, Theme with dark/light + dynamic color API 31+, Typography)

**Configuration:**
- KSP enabled for Room compilation (faster than KAPT)
- Kotlin serialization for type-safe Navigation 2.8+ routes
- Room schema export to disk for migration validation
- Java 11 source/target compatibility

**Missing (to be implemented):**
- No Composable screen files yet (all screen UI code)
- No Navigation graph/route definitions
- App widget code not present
- No export/share implementations
